column9: RCA promotional release in generic promo slipcover. These all use the same standard base design with b&w RCA/Columbia logo in top left, and the label says "(C) Artwork and Design 1986" regardless of the year the film came out (even some post 1986, such as Punch Line). Actual distribution date of these promos seems to be more like 1989/90, but it's possible some were produced earlier. Need more info.

column9: In Sony.1986.8 and Sony.1986.11 (Aug & Nov 1986) fliers, this title is listed with its 00HS- catalog number but original ¥13,800 price. Typically the A- series had the full price, but I had thought the switch to 00HS- numbers happened at the same time as the price drops (with factory sticker added to the outer shrink). column9: Note: RCA tapes with an 00HS- prefix catalog# and a round number price of ¥12,000, ¥13,000, ¥14,000 typically have barcodes and the 00HS number is printed on the spine. These were (mostly?) released in 1989, shortly before the switch to CSWF- numbering and the blue border re-releases, and price major price drops to ¥3,800 that RCA and Warner both did the same year. This is evidenced by the fact that the first CSWF- releases from RCA were also in 1989, and some still have a ¥13,000 price, presumably earlier in the year before the switch, while others were ¥3,800, presumably later in the year. In other words, 1989 was the transitional year between both pricing and catalog numbering for RCA.
